sub: Every _project/ artifact is one ProjectEntity — Identity · State · Relation, reduced from five per-kind interfaces to three primitives. The center is the contract; each petal is an entity kind; the last slot is the tooling it feeds. Project-agnostic: the kinds, enums, and copy all come from a per-project config.
axis: 1 CONTRACT → 5 KINDS → VALIDATOR · SCAFFOLDER · GUARD → DERIVED DASHBOARD
sourceLine: source-of-truth: schema/project-entity.base.schema.json + project-system's validated _project/ entities · generated by tools/render-hub.mjs
strategy: One contract, three primitives, many disposable renderers — the planning layer reduced the way a UI reduces to State and Relation. A good reduction deletes fields; this one dropped five interfaces to three.

domains.name: ProjectEntity
domains.note: One non-optional contract every _project/ file derives from — Identity (kind·id·title) · State (status·updated) · Relation (links). kind & id are loader-derived from the path; the authored surface is just title/status/updated (+links/tags). The validator, scaffolder, guard, and this dashboard all read this single source.

domains.name: ADR ledger
domains.note: 3 decision entities — status over the per-kind enum (all accepted). 2 typed links touch this kind.

domains.name: Retro log
domains.note: 1 report entity — status over the per-kind enum (all complete). 2 typed links touch this kind.

domains.name: Build plans
domains.note: 1 pipeline entity — status over the per-kind enum (all design). 0 typed links touch this kind.

domains.name: Plans
domains.note: 1 roadmap entity — status over the per-kind enum (all proposed). 1 typed link touch this kind.

domains.name: Work log
domains.note: 1 session entity — status over the per-kind enum (all active). 1 typed link touch this kind.

domains.name: Triad
domains.note: Three zero-dependency engines, all reading the one contract via lib/contract.mjs (no check re-implemented): the validator (per-kind enums, link resolution, prose↔frontmatter), the scaffolder behind /new-<kind>, and the PreToolUse guard that blocks any _project/ write that would break the contract.

banner.text: Self-sustaining: new files are born valid, invalid writes are blocked at save time — for any project that supplies a config.
